act-caitlin-tough-brindabella.summary: As ACT Labor candidate for Brindabella in the 2024 election, Tough provided a candidate statement to Canberrans for a Good Death adopting the ACT Labor statement, which expressed support for the ACT's voluntary assisted dying legislation and noted all ACT Labor MLAs voted in favour of it. (Stated position; she was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

sources.title: Appendix: Responses from Individual Candidates - Statement by Caitlin Tough, ACT Labor candidate for Brindabella

(Stated position; she was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

act-thomas-emerson-kurrajong.summary: In response to the Canberrans for a Good Death 2024 election candidate survey, Emerson (Independents for Canberra, Kurrajong) answered 'Yes' to the question 'Do you support VAD', and supported widening eligibility and a binding advance directive in the next Assembly term. (Stated position; he was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

sources.title: Report on the survey of candidates for the 2024 ACT Election and their attitudes to widening eligibility for voluntary assisted dying

act-yvette-berry-ginninderra.summary: Voted in favour of the Voluntary Assisted Dying Bill 2023, which passed the ACT Legislative Assembly 20 votes to 5 on 5 June 2024 (recorded in the Ayes).

sources.title: Debates Weekly Hansard, Tenth Assembly, 5 June 2024

nt-gerard-maley-nelson.summary: Then-Acting Chief Minister Maley said he supports the principles of voluntary assisted dying but would ask his constituents before voting, and confirmed members would be allowed a conscience vote. No recorded vote has occurred. Stated position.

sources.title: Half of Top End parliamentarians voice support for assisted dying

nt-justine-davis-johnston.summary: Independent member Davis supports voluntary assisted dying; she moved the original NT Legislative Assembly motion (13 May 2025) to progress VAD that led to the parliamentary inquiry, and chairs the NT VAD working group. No recorded vote has occurred. Stated position.

sources.title: NT inquiry finds strong support for voluntary assisted dying
NT CLP commits to VAD vote, rejects Labor's bill

nt-robyn-lambley-araluen.summary: Independent member and Assembly Speaker Lambley opposes proceeding with voluntary assisted dying in the NT, saying 'I don't think we're ready. Maybe we'll never be ready in the Territory' and 'It will be a disaster... We just need to take it at our own pace,' citing difficulties educating remote Indigenous communities. No recorded vote has occurred. Stated position.

sources.title: Australia's Northern Territory considers passing assisted dying laws for second time in 31 years
Parties commit to conscience vote on NT euthanasia bill

nt-selena-uibo-arnhem.summary: Labor Opposition Leader Uibo supports voluntary assisted dying; on 13 October 2025 she submitted a draft VAD bill to the government, urging it to adopt the legislation rather than 'waste more time'. The government declined to use her bill. No recorded vote has occurred. Stated position.

sources.title: NT CLP commits to VAD vote, rejects Labor's bill
